title: ApprovalEvidence
description: Forge-proven eligible-approval evidence for a require-review obligation (ADR-0017 §3, OQ-23). This is the only contract require-review may be satisfied against — a resolved discussion/challenge thread is acknowledgement, not authorization, and has no representable path into this schema (GitLab dossier §4). Safety-bearing: unknown fields reject at every level (ADR-0017 §9).

observedAt.description: When this evidence was fetched from the forge.

expiresAt.description: Arming precondition (ADR-0017 §4): evidence observed but expired by decision time is not valid approval.

verifyingCapability.description: The forge capability that proved this evidence. "none" is the documented capability-gap value (D-017 B5): missing capability must never auto-merge and must never silently downgrade to a challenge/thread proxy — it is representable only as the fail-closed shape enforced below, never as an ordinary (if empty) evidence record.

then.description: Capability gap: principal/source/eligibility must be structurally absent, not merely empty, so a DecisionRecord consuming this evidence can only represent fail-closed, never a satisfied or silently-downgraded obligation (D-017 B5, REQ-P3-E1-S04-03).

isAuthor.description: Self-approval policy (ADR-0015 §5, GitLab dossier §4 'MR-author approval'): the approving principal can never be the change's author. The adapter must verify forge-side enforcement (e.g. merge_requests_author_approval: false) and independently exclude client-side (defense in depth, since the setting is overridable per-MR). Declaring isAuthor: true is not representable — a self-approval can never be encoded as valid evidence, whether or not the forge setting was actually enforced.

rule.description: The approval rule name or CODEOWNERS entry that proved eligibility (GitLab dossier §4 step (a)).

ruleType.description: Closed set of forge approval-rule kinds (GitLab dossier §4, rules[].rule_type). Deliberately excludes any discussion/challenge-resolution kind: ADR-0017 §3 treats a resolved discussion as acknowledgement, never authorization, so there is no enum value a discussion resolver could ever populate this field with.

eligibleApproverIds.description: Forge-proven eligible-approver set for source.rule (GitLab dossier §4 step (b), rules[].eligible_approvers[].id). Must never be populated from a rule GitLab auto-approved because it was impossible to satisfy (only eligible approver is the author; no eligible approvers; required > eligible) — dossier §4 'Invalid-rule pitfall': such a rule has approved: true with an empty/insufficient approved_by, and empty eligibility can never stand in for real evidence.

pins.description: Reuses DecisionRecord's pin shape (P3-E1-S02): approval evidence is only valid for the exact source/target/merge-result state it was observed against, never a movable ref (ADR-0017 §1). Pairs with observedAt/expiresAt against dossier §4 'Staleness' — approvals reset on new push, so a pinned sourceSha that predates the current head is stale evidence, not valid evidence.
